items.briefing:
Cyber threats across the Asia-Pacific are increasing, driven by AI-enabled attacks and geopolitical tensions — a direct concern for Australian organisations. Locally, a new warning highlights that the Australian government's Essential Eight security framework may not adequately protect against phishing attacks, meaning staff vigilance remains critical even if your organisation follows compliance guidelines. Australian businesses are responding by consolidating their security tools onto unified platforms to better manage growing risks.

If your organisation runs a WordPress website, be aware of a newly discovered vulnerability called WP2Shell that could allow attackers to take full remote control — ensure your WordPress plugins and core software are updated immediately.

featured.title: PhishByte warns Essential Eight misses phishing risk
featured.quote: Essential Eight compliance doesn't protect against phishing, creating false security for Australian businesses.
